name: Hispanic Association Of Contractors And Enterprises
description_short: HACE CDC is a Philadelphia-based nonprofit providing free housing counseling, tax preparation, and community development services to low-to-moderate income residents in Eastern North Philadelphia since 1982.
description_long:
Hispanic Association Of Contractors And Enterprises (HACE) is a community development corporation established in 1982 that operates in Eastern North Philadelphia. The organization focuses on building equitable neighborhoods through affordable housing development, commercial space creation, and supportive community services. HACE serves predominantly Latino and low-to-moderate income residents and small business owners in the region.

HACE offers multiple free services directly addressing consumer finance needs. The organization provides HUD-approved housing counseling through trained staff and operates a free tax preparation program for qualifying low-to-moderate income households (contact: 215-426-8025 or 215-437-7867). Additionally, HACE established the Good Lands Trust, a community land trust model that helps low-income families achieve affordable homeownership by acquiring and redeveloping vacant properties. Homeowners gain access to free maintenance programs and loan products after purchase.

HACE distinguishes itself through deep community integration and a comprehensive neighborhood development approach. Rather than offering isolated financial services, the organization combines housing counseling with affordable housing development, small business support, community engagement activities, and partnerships with local organizations. Their Good Lands Trust model specifically targets first-time homebuyers with incomes below 50% of area median income, making homeownership accessible to populations typically excluded from traditional lending. The organization demonstrates commitment through active community projects, volunteer coordination, and participation in policy advocacy.

As a nonprofit with legitimate 501(c)(3) status and established track record since 1982, HACE appears genuinely committed to serving underbanked communities. However, the website provides limited details on specific eligibility criteria for services, application timelines, or service capacity. Potential clients should contact directly to confirm availability and qualifications. The organization focuses primarily on Eastern North Philadelphia residents, limiting geographic reach for those outside this area.
pros: Free HUD-approved housing counseling provided by trained staff
Free tax preparation services for qualifying low-to-moderate income households
Good Lands Trust offers affordable homeownership to first-time buyers with incomes below 50% AMI
Community land trust homes are 10x less likely to face foreclosure
Established nonprofit with 40+ year history (since 1982) and 501(c)(3) status
Integrated community development approach combining counseling with affordable housing development
Partnerships with Philadelphia Foundation and Pennsylvania Department of Community and Economic Development
cons: Services appear limited to Eastern North Philadelphia residents, limiting geographic accessibility
Website lacks specific eligibility criteria, application process details, and service availability/wait times
Limited information on housing counseling scope, duration, or specialized services offered
No online application or intake process described; requires phone contact for information
Good Lands Trust projects appear limited in number (approximately 250 CLTs nationwide suggests constrained capacity)
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Eastern North Philadelphia with low-to-moderate incomes seeking affordable mortgage programs
Low-income households needing free tax preparation services during tax season
Renters and homeowners in North Philadelphia seeking HUD-approved housing counseling
diagnosis: HACE is best for Eastern North Philadelphia residents with low-to-moderate incomes seeking free housing counseling, tax preparation, or affordable homeownership opportunities through community land trust models. The main caveat is limited geographic service area and lack of online service information; prospective clients must contact directly to confirm eligibility and availability.
services: Free tax preparation for low-to-moderate income households
Good Lands Trust community land trust homeownership program
Post-purchase homeowner support and maintenance programs
Commercial space development for small businesses
Small business support and economic development services
Community engagement and resident advocacy programs
Community land trust property acquisition and redevelopment
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
